expectations.text: The agent edited chatbot.py to add @observe or start_observation to answer_question
expectations.evidence: chatbot.py line 27: '@px.observe(name="answer_question")' decorates the answer_question function directly.

expectations.text: The agent added enable_storage() to chatbot.py (not just px.init())
expectations.evidence: chatbot.py lines 7-10: 'from pixie import enable_storage' followed by 'enable_storage()' at module level.

expectations.text: The agent created a test file (test_*.py) with assert_dataset_pass or assert_pass
expectations.evidence: tests/test_rag_chatbot.py imports and calls 'assert_dataset_pass' in both test_factuality and test_answer_relevancy.

expectations.text: The test file calls enable_storage() inside the runnable (not at module level in the test)
expectations.evidence: tests/test_rag_chatbot.py lines 27-32: 'def runnable(eval_input):' contains 'enable_storage()' as its first statement, inside the function body.

expectations.text: The agent wrote code or commands to create a named dataset (DatasetStore().create() or 'pixie dataset create' CLI)
expectations.evidence: build_dataset.py line 45: 'store.create(DATASET_NAME)' creates the 'rag-chatbot-golden' dataset. MEMORY.md also shows 'pixie dataset create rag-chatbot-golden' CLI command.

expectations.text: The agent wrote code or commands to save traces to the dataset ('pixie dataset save' CLI or DatasetStore().append())
expectations.evidence: build_dataset.py lines 48-57: 'store.append(DATASET_NAME, Evaluable(...))' saves each golden item. MEMORY.md also shows 'pixie dataset save' CLI commands.

expectations.text: The agent created a MEMORY.md or notes file in the project directory
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md exists at /project/MEMORY.md and contains detailed project documentation including entry points, instrumentation notes, dataset schema, eval plan, and commands.

claims.claim: No real LLM calls are made, so there is no last_llm_call span in traces
claims.evidence: chatbot.py confirms the mock uses keyword matching, not an LLM. MEMORY.md explicitly notes 'from_trace=root' is used because there is no LLM span.

claims.claim: The dataset contains 4 factual QA items
claims.evidence: build_dataset.py defines golden_items with exactly 4 entries covering capitals, language, population, and currency.

claims.claim: enable_storage() is called at module import time in chatbot.py

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the dataset was actually created and persisted (e.g., that pixie_datasets/ contains a valid JSON file). The build_dataset.py script is present but no assertion verifies it was run or produced a valid dataset.

suggestions.assertion: The agent wrote code or commands to save traces to the dataset ('pixie dataset save' CLI or DatasetStore().append())
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes because build_dataset.py uses DatasetStore().append(), but the evaluables saved have eval_output pre-set (not from live traces). A stronger assertion would check that traces from running the app were saved, not just hardcoded golden answers.

eval_feedback.overall: All 7 assertions pass with clear evidence. The with_skill run produces a well-structured solution. Consider adding assertions that verify dataset contents are valid (not null outputs) and that the test actually exercises the runnable.
